text: Wa yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a gisi ka m suataŋa. Fi baga a dak ba le ta ba piisaŋa a bo juijui ate ŋa a dɛ la?>>

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Wa nidoabilisaŋa ale wa liema a jam ayen ba yik wa ate wa sui yɔk, alege wa zɛri. Wa weeni ayen, <<Aaya, mi a kum kama ayen m ga siŋ vɔrup a cheŋ m biika jigi.>> Dila wa kowa a yaa kum wa biika.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Alege Juda bikpagni Ɛri le jam nye diila a bie kama Naawen nimbie po, dila nyiŋ Naawen yaa ko wa.

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: Wa yaa jam pai garukku a dueni ka wa tɛŋ ate wa kpagni a ga jam yeri.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: ate wa yaa pai ba a nyo ligrɔma kpagni nisima po, juijui dek ate ba jam lik Josefi la.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Diila ba fɛ mi kama a ta nyini Yiburu demma tɛŋka a nyini, ate dela mɛ mi an nye wari ya a magsi ba a nyo mu likka dɔk po.>>

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: Ku saluiku wa popola yaa ta wa a daani, ate wa tom wi sampoporisaŋa ale yam nyama meena Ejipiti tɛŋka po. Faaro yaa weeni ba wa gɔdaasimaŋa alege waai a karo a weeni ŋa kiri a sak wa.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Ate wie a sum tulim nye a magsi chaab ale wa ale weeni ti diila. Mi ŋman pilim nya m kpaŋtaŋa kama, ate nuru nwala ba pai wa a yik tiib nyiŋ.>>

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Josefi yaa tulisi Faaro ayen, <<Mi an baga a nye, alege Wen ale te Faaro weeni ŋaai ate wa a yaali la.>>

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Alege ŋa le poom a nɛ ŋa a nueri la, waai an baga a weeni ayen ŋa nɛ jaaboa. Ŋa lɔrima ase ka ŋa le poom ka diila. Ate mi yaa yiti.
